title: allocasuarina luehmannii
intro: Allocasuarina luehmannii, also known as Luehmann's sheoak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Casuarinaceae. It is a small to medium-sized tree native to Western Australia, known for its conical shape and sharp, needle-like leaves. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Allocasuarina luehmannii include treating skin conditions, wound healing, and potentially reducing fever, with herbal preparations often involving the bark being made into infusions, tinctures, or poultices.
